description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a long, rectangular footprint and a low-pitched, dark brown gable roof. The exterior walls are clad in a light tan or beige material, likely brick or stone, with a series of evenly spaced windows along the longer facades. Two prominent chimneys and several roof vents are visible, suggesting multiple fireplaces or heating systems inside. The front entrance is accessible via a paved walkway that leads to a modest stoop, and the building sits on a neatly maintained green lawn. The shorter ends of the structure feature open gable ends, with one side showing a partially sheltered area that could serve as a carport or covered patio. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of mid-century or ranch-style suburban homes.
description: A single-story, rectangular bungalow with a dark gable roof, tan brick walls, and multiple chimneys, set on a green lawn with a paved walkway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, paved walkway, and a row of windows along the facade.
description_view_1: Side gable end with an open area beneath the roof, possibly a carport or covered patio.
description_view_2: Rear view with a similar row of windows and visible chimneys, but no main entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end, also open, mirroring the structure seen in view 1.
